meta.title: Vancouver Island Campgrounds — BC Parks & Recreation Sites and Trails BC (RSTBC)
meta.scope: Vancouver Island only (Gulf Islands, Quadra, Cortes, Denman, Hornby, and Lasqueti excluded from this pass)
meta.completeness_note: This is a working v1 list, not exhaustive. BC Parks entries cover all known vehicle-accessible frontcountry campgrounds on Vancouver Island proper. RSTBC has 1,200+ recreation sites province-wide with hundreds on Vancouver Island; only a well-documented subset with published site counts is included here. Expand over time via sitesandtrailsbc.ca and bcparks.ca.

bc_parks.name: Goldstream Provincial Park
bc_parks.location: 16 km NW of Victoria, Trans-Canada Hwy
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservable in peak season with some first-come-first-served sites; winter camping FCFS with limited services

bc_parks.name: Juan de Fuca Provincial Park (China Beach Campground)
bc_parks.location: Southwest Vancouver Island, Juan de Fuca coast
bc_parks.reservation_status: Nearly all sites reservable; 1 site FCFS

bc_parks.name: Rathtrevor Beach Provincial Park
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservable May–October; limited winter FCFS sites (mid-Oct to mid-March, reduced services)

bc_parks.name: Gordon Bay Provincial Park
bc_parks.reservation_status: 123 of 126 sites reservable; limited winter FCFS at reduced rate

bc_parks.name: Elk Falls Provincial Park
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservable; open March 20–Oct 31 plus year-round winter camping

bc_parks.name: Miracle Beach Provincial Park
bc_parks.location: Black Creek, between Campbell River and Courtenay
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservable; open March 20–Oct 31, winter camping March 1–19 & Nov 1–30

bc_parks.name: Morton Lake Provincial Park
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservable; open April 30–Sept 27 plus year-round winter camping

bc_parks.name: Strathcona Provincial Park — Buttle Lake Campground
bc_parks.location: Highway 28, ~48 km west of Campbell River
bc_parks.reservation_status: Reservation-only in recent seasons; unreserved sites revert to first-come-first-served nightly

bc_parks.name: Strathcona Provincial Park — Ralph River Campground
bc_parks.location: 35 km south of Buttle Lake along Buttle Lake (Western Mines) Road

bc_parks_known_gaps: Cowichan River Provincial Park (Stolz Falls & 66 Mile Trestle campgrounds)
Cape Scott Provincial Park (backcountry only, no vehicle camping)
Newcastle Island Marine Provincial Park (boat access only, off Nanaimo)

rstbc.name: Nahwitti Lake Recreation Site
rstbc.location: En route to/from Cape Scott Provincial Park, North Island

rstbc.name: Marble River Recreation Site
rstbc.location: 40 min drive from Port McNeill, adjacent to Marble River Provincial Park

rstbc.name: Spring Salmon Place Recreation Site (Horne Lake Potholes)
Lizard Lake Recreation Site
rstbc.reservation_status: Just over half reservable (60-day rolling window), remainder first-come-first-served; $20/night

rstbc.name: Pacific Yew Recreation Site
rstbc.location: Mohun Lake, Sayward Canoe Route, north of Campbell River

rstbc.name: Upper Campbell Reservoir Recreation Site
rstbc.location: Upper Campbell Lake, near Strathcona Park

rstbc.name: Blackies Beach Recreation Site
rstbc.location: Backroads toward Nahmint Lake, near Sproat Lake

rstbc.name: Scout Beach Recreation Site
rstbc.location: Great Central Lake, near Port Alberni

rstbc.name: Mr. Canoehead Recreation Site
rstbc.location: Sayward Forest Canoe Route, between Amor Lake and Surprise Lake

rstbc.name: Schoen Lake area sites (Western Forest Products, upper & lower)
rstbc.location: Near Woss, adjacent to Schoen Lake Provincial Park

rstbc_known_gaps: Nitnaht Lake area recreation sites (some run by Nitinaht campground keepers, booked online)
Numerous Sayward Forest / Campbell Lake area recreation sites north of Loveland Bay
Nanaimo Lakes (First, Second, Third, Fourth Lake) recreation sites
Woss Lake and Nimpkish Lake area sites beyond Kinman Creek
Full province-wide RSTBC dataset available via sitesandtrailsbc.ca search/interactive map for further expansion
